info.title: REST API
info.description:
Everything you need to connect to an external eCommerce platform or other third party products requiring a two way integration. Can be used in combination with the oData API.

Api supports authentication using Apikey, which is can be created in the Front Systems portal under Admin|Integration users.

post.description: Inserts a new bonus transaction for a customer. Bonustransaction can be both postive (earn bonus) and negative (use bonus)

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

put.description:
This API endpoint is designed to either create a new customer record or update an existing one based on a unique identifier. The hierarchy of the unique identifier used is as follows:

1. Customer ID: If a Customer ID is provided, it will be used as the primary unique identifier to either create a new customer or update an existing one.

2. ExtID (External customer ID): In the absence of a Customer ID, the API will use the ExtID as the unique identifier.

3. Phone Number: If neither a Customer ID nor an ExtID is provided, the customer's phone number will be used to identify the customer record.

4. Email: If none of the above identifiers are available, the customer's email address will be used as the last resort for identifying the customer record.

In the case of an update, if a match is found based on the provided identifier, the corresponding customer's record will be updated with the new data. If no match is found, a new customer record will be created.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

requestBody.description: if key field is included in the body, this will be used to look up existing customer to update, Key fields sorted by priority: 1) customerid (Front Systems internal customer ID)  -   2) extid (External customer ID)   -  3) email   -  4) phone

promotionsOffers.name: Acme Summer Sale
promotionsOffers.description: Get 20% off on all Acme products during the summer sale.

post.description:
Inserts a new giftcard transaction. If giftcardToken does not exists, a new giftcard will be created.

To determine what store to register these giftcards from you might need to set header parameter x-storeId to f.ex. storeId of the webshop.

parameters.name: giftcardToken
parameters.description: If giftcardToken does not exists, a new giftcard will be created.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

parameters.name: x-storeId
parameters.description: Indicates which store the giftcards origin should be registered through. Its significant to ensure that the giftcard can only be used in the same region.

post.description: [obsolete, Apikey key authentication should be used instead] All other endpoints in the rest api relies on a valid authorization token. This token can be obtained from the login endpoint

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

post.description: This endpoint allows you to update an existing pricelist or create a new one if it does not already exist.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

requestBody.description: In the price details, either productid (Front Systems’ internal identifier at the variant level) or gtin (identifier at the SKU level) can be used. For external integrators, gtin is recommended as productid is typically not available to them. Prices are managed at the product level, and the gtin serves solely as an identifier to match the appropriate product for applying the specified price.

parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

put.description: Insert an expected delivery (previously known as purchase order). The delivery must be received in the Deliveries screen in Portal before any stock movements are made.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

requestBody.description:
NB: When the value of "insertAsConfirmed" is set to true, it indicates that the delivery has been confirmed but requires manual receiving intervention from employees. On the other hand, when the value of "insertAsConfirmed" is set to false, it implies that the delivery is a direct transfer, and there is no need for manual receiving by employees.

Please be aware that the "cost" field in the payload pertains to the cost per product, regardless of its sizes, or associated GTINs. Our system supports cost calculation on a per-product basis, rather than accounting for each size/GTIN.

put.description:
This is for sending inn sale. The important this here is that the sum of price in sales lines is equal to the sum of amount in payment lines. If a product is discounted use the discounted price in sales lines. The discount rate will be calculated based on the products outprice (full price).

SaleDateTime is expected as CET.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

parameters.name: x-storeId
parameters.description: Indicates where the sale is to be registered. Needs to be set if API key is not connected to only one store.

example.comment: This is a sale, a return would have negative qty and payment.

put.description:
Update product attributes on the variant level. 

Size system is only possible to update for products without any stockmovements yet. If you have to change sizesystem for a product with stockmovements, the only option is to delete it and create a new one.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: You can use an extId value here instead if those suites your integration better.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

requestBody.description:
Product group/sub group, brand, season and color attributes will be automatically created if they don't exists.

When updating groupName, subGroupName must be included in body as well.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a product. The product will still be included in revenue reports etc, but marked as deleted in the product catalog and not possible to process in apis, POS etc.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: You can use an extId value here instead if those suites your integration better.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

post.description:
Create a new product on variant level. 

Read more about how to populate product master data in the API guide: 
https://developer.frontsystems.com/guides/masterdata#nHQrC

Using extId and productId that already exists in the system will then update that product instead of creating a new one.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

get.description:
Orders from e-commerce platform to be handled in physical store. POS will pick up the order and provide instructions to the store associates. 

POS (v 1.0.38 public preview) is currently supporting the following order types:
- Buy online, pick up in store
- Reserve online, buy in store

Use this endpoint to get an existing order.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

put.description:
Orders from e-commerce platform to be handled in physical store. POS will pick up the order and provide instructions to the store associates.

POS is currently supporting the following order types:

* Buy online, pick up in store 
* Buy online, ship from store 
* Reserve online, buy in store
* Buy online, return in store. (Does not use this endpoint) 

Use this endpoint to update existing orders. They will be instantly updated in the POS. Subscribe to webhooks for real-time updates of the orders.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

delete.description:
Orders from e-commerce platform to be handled in physical store. POS will pick up the order and provide instructions to the store associates. 

POS (v 1.0.38 public preview) is currently supporting the following order types:
- Buy online, pick up in store
- Reserve online, buy in store

Use this endpoint to delete an order. It will then disappear from the POS.

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

post.description:
Orders from e-commerce platform to be handled in physical store. POS will pick up the order and provide instructions to the store associates.

POS is currently supporting the following order types:

* Buy online, pick up in store 
* Buy online, ship from store 
* Reserve online, buy in store
* Buy online, return in store. (Does not use this endpoint) 

Use this endpoint to add new orders. Subscribe to webhooks for real-time updates of the orders.

parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

parameters.name: x-StockId

The reserved items will be reflected in all on hand quantity endpoints, which is useful when selling items in multiple channels from the same stock/warehouse. Items should be released when sales are registered.

post.description:
The `bulk-insert` endpoint is designed to allow clients to **create multiple new products** in a single API call. It is optimized for initial product catalog imports or large-scale onboarding of new items.

---

### Request Format
The request body should be a JSON array of product objects. Each product should include key attributes such as:

- extId: External identifier used to match existing products.
- name, number, variant, brand, color, season, sizeSystem*.
- productPrices: List of price entries per pricelist.
- productSizes: Each size must include a valid gtin and label*.
- pictureRef: A URL to the product image.

⚠️ Note: If createProductSpecificSizeSystem is set to true, you can name the label as you wish since it is only linked to this product. If not, a valid label for chosen sizeSystem must be used.

⚠️ Note: The total payload size must not exceed 2 MB. If you need to upsert more products, consider batching them into multiple requests.

### Response Format
The response includes:

- created: Number of newly created products.
- unprocessedProducts: List of products that failed to process, with error details.

example.name: ACME Running Shoes
example.internalDescription: Trail running shoes with breathable mesh.

put.description:
The `bulk-upsert` endpoint allows clients to **create or update multiple products** in a single API call. This is especially useful for synchronizing product catalogs between systems, ensuring that new products are added and existing ones are updated efficiently.

---

### Request Format

The request body should be a JSON array of product objects. Each product should include key attributes such as:

- `extId`: External identifier used to match existing products.
- `name`, `number`, `variant`, `brand`, `color`, `season`, `sizeSystem`*.
- `productPrices`: List of price entries per pricelist.
- `productSizes`: Each size must include a valid `gtin` and `label`*.
- `pictureRef`: A URL to the product image.

> ⚠️ **Note:** If createProductSpecificSizeSystem is set to true, you can name the label as you wish since it is only linked to this product. If not, a valid label for chosen sizeSystem must be used.

> ⚠️ **Note:** The total payload size must not exceed **2 MB**. If you need to upsert more products, consider batching them into multiple requests.


### Response Format
The response includes:

- created: Number of newly created products.
- updated: Number of successfully updated products.
- unprocessedProducts: List of products that failed to process, with error details.

example.name: ACME Running Shoes
example.internalDescription: Trail running shoes with breathable mesh.

200.description:
Success
(**See tab two for partial success**)

get.description:
Request an array of orders based on various filtering options. 

Ordertype overview: 
	1.	BOPIS (Buy Online, Pick Up In Store)
	2.	BOSS (Buy Online, Ship to Store)
	3.	ROPIS (Reserve Online, Pick Up In Store)
	4.	Return - Refund or exchange 
	5.	Kiosk - Order through in-store kiosk solution
	6.	Service - Orders for repairs or services
	7.	Warehouse - Fulfilled directly from warehouse
	8.	Reservation - Hold item without payment upfront
	9.	SecondHand - Pre-owned products

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Format - int32.

get.description:
# Guide for Sales Aggregates API

The Sales Aggregates API provides access to consolidated sales data with flexible grouping options. This endpoint enables reporting systems and business intelligence tools to retrieve pre-aggregated sales metrics without the need for complex real-time calculations.

## Overview

The Aggregates API returns sales data grouped by various dimensions such as Brand, Employee, Gender, Product Group, Time (Hours), or Point of Sale. Data is aggregated nightly and includes key metrics like sale counts, quantities, totals, costs, discounts, and VAT amounts.

**Important Note:** Aggregate data is processed nightly, so sales made during the current day will not be available until the next day. For real-time sales data, use the Sales API endpoint instead.

## Retrieve Sales Aggregates

Use the Aggregates endpoint to retrieve aggregated sales data for reporting and analytics purposes. The data should be fetched based on your reporting requirements, using appropriate date ranges and grouping parameters to optimize performance.

### Groupings

**Available Groupings:**
- `Brand` - Group by product brand
- `Empl` - Group by employee/salesperson
- `Gender` - Group by product gender category
- `Group` - Group by product group
- `Hours` - Group by hour of sale
- `Pos` - Group by point of sale terminal

## Store Access Control

The API automatically filters data based on user permissions:

- **Custom Store Filter:** Use the `X-StoreId` header to filter results for a specific store

**Example with Store Filter:**
```
GET .../api/Aggregates?fromDate=2023-10-01&toDate=2023-10-31
X-StoreId: 101
```

## Usage Examples

### Weekly Employee Performance Report
```
GET .../api/Aggregates?fromDate=2023-10-09&toDate=2023-10-15&groupings=Empl
```

### Monthly Sales by Product Group and Gender
```
GET .../api/Aggregates?fromDate=2023-10-01&toDate=2023-10-31&groupings=Group,Gender
```

## Data Freshness and Timing

**Important:** The aggregates data is processed through a nightly batch job. This means:

- Sales made today will not appear in aggregate data until tomorrow
- The most recent available data is from yesterday 
- Aggregate data is optimized for historical reporting and trend analysis

## Best Practices

1. **Date Range Optimization:** Use specific date ranges to improve performance and reduce data transfer
2. **Selective Groupings:** Specify only the grouping dimensions you need rather than using all defaults
3. **Caching:** Consider caching results on the client side since aggregate data changes only once daily
4. **Store Filtering:** Use the X-StoreId header when building store-specific reports

## Integration Considerations

When integrating the Aggregates API into reporting systems:

- Schedule data pulls to run early in the morning after nightly processing is complete
- Build dashboards that clearly indicate the data refresh timing to end users

---

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: Start date for the report period. Defaults to today

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: End date for the report period. Defaults to today

parameters.name: groupings
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of grouping dimensions. Defaults to all available groupings

parameters.name: x-storeId
parameters.description: Limits the response to only one store

200.description:
Successful request with data

**Example Request:**
```
GET /api/Aggregates?fromDate=2023-10-01&toDate=2023-10-31&groupings=Brand,Empl
```
## Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `saleDate` | DateTime | Date of the sales data |
| `companyId` | Integer | Company identifier |
| `storeId` | Integer | Store identifier |
| `storeName` | String | Store name |
| `saleCount` | Integer | Number of individual sales transactions |
| `qty` | Decimal | Total quantity of items sold |
| `lineTotal` | Decimal | Total sales amount including VAT |
| `cost` | Decimal | Total cost of goods sold |
| `discount` | Decimal | Total discount amount applied |
| `vat` | Decimal | Total VAT amount |
| `colValue` | String | The value for the grouping dimension (e.g., brand name, employee ID) |
| `colName` | String | The grouping dimension name |

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: You can use an productId or productGuid values here

parameters.name: gtin

parameters.name: gtin

get.description: Retrieves a single stock count batch with all line details by ID

parameters.name: stockCountBatchId
parameters.description: The stock count batch identifier

get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of stock count batch summaries

parameters.name: pageNumber
parameters.description: The page number (1-based). Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of items per page. Defaults to 25.

delete.description: Soft-deletes all GTINs for a single product by productid.

parameters.name: productId
parameters.description: You can use an productId or productGuid values here

parameters.name: x-api-key
parameters.description: Authorization. Apikey can be generated in Front Systems Portal|Admin|Integration users

ins.description: Insert timestamp - when the batch was created

insBy.description: Username of the person who created the count

extStockId.description: External stock identifier for mapping to external systems

isReconciled.description: Whether the stock count has been reconciled (differences resolved)

StockCountBatchResponseDto.description:
Represents a complete stock count batch with all line details
Used for the single item GET endpoint

ins.description: Insert timestamp - when the batch was created

insBy.description: Username of the person who created the count

extStockId.description: External stock identifier for mapping to external systems

isReconciled.description: Whether the stock count has been reconciled (differences resolved)

itemsCounted.description: Total number of stock count lines in this batch

StockCountBatchSummaryDto.description:
Represents a summary of a stock count batch without line details
Used for the list endpoint with pagination

lineText.description: Product description text (e.g., "Winter Jacket Blue / L")

ins.description: Timestamp when the line was created

insBy.description: Username of the person who counted this line

isValid.description: Indicates if the line passed validation rules

StockCountDetailDto.description: Represents a single line/detail in a stock count batch

isCompleteStockCount.description: If true, inventory levels on all other items than included in the request will be adjusted to 0

extId.description: External ID of the store in Front Systems (ofter reffered to as StoreNo). If not present, the store from the header x-api-key or x-StoreId will be used

validFrom.description: If date not specified, MinDate(01/01/2000) will be used. If time is not specified, 0:00:00 will be used

validTo.description: If date not specified, MaxDate(31/12/9999) will be used. If time is not specified, 0:00:00 will be used

pricelistId.description: Front Systems internal price list ID. If not defined, name property will be used as identifier

extRef.description: F.ex. used as giftcard id when paying with giftcard(5)

paymentType.description: Invoice = 6, PayEx = 7, Vipps = 10, NetAxept = 115, Dibs = 116, COD = 117, KlarnaFaktura = 124,  KlarnaKonto = 125,  Collector = 141,  GiftCard = 5,  BonusPoints = 128

cardType.description: None = 0, Visa = 3, ECMC = 4, Amex = 5, Diners = 6, BankAxxess = 10000

responseBody.description: When a web payment is used, this field should be populated with the response of the web payment request preferably containing orderid, current status, transactionid and timestamp, f.ex the response of the call to Vipps API.

createProductSpecificSize.description: If true a sizesystem will be created and linked to this product and its variants only, so changes to sizes only would affect this product alone.

id.description: Optional strict UUID identificator that can be sent by customer and then used for updating product instead of our internal productId in our product CRUD endpoints.

extId.description: Optional identificator that can be sent by customer and then used for updating product instead of our internal productId in our product CRUD endpoints.

productid.description: Internal front systems product id which is defined on the product variant level.

variant.description: Color code (Variant) Eg. 00001. Typically productNumber + Color code is the unique VariantID in your master data source. M12345600001

tags.description: Product tags in a comma separate list

price.description: Selling price. Only to be used if you have same price and currency for all stores within the company. Use pricelists for more flexible pricing of products

isStockProduct.description: If product is kept in stock or not. For some customers, plastic bags are not stocked products (default true)

pictureRef.description: Picture displayed in search view. Not updatable.

isWebAvailable.description: True if product is available online

sizeSystem.description: If 'createProductSpecificSize' is false, a pre-defined size chart (SizeSystem) is used. If not specified, the SizeSystem with best match will be used.

productSizes.description: Array with product sizes, gtins and externalSKUs

images.description: Array of product images - full URL. Max 10 images, max 10 MB size per image.

cost.description: Cost price. Only to be used if you have same cost price and currency for all stores within the company. Use cost price list per stock for more flexible cost price handling.

isNoLabel.description: Used if a product should not have a label. eg for small rings etc. If an RFID solution is used, these items will not be included in RFID stock taking

requestId.description: An optional, client-generated unique identifier (GUID) that ensures idempotent processing of requests, preventing duplicate operations if the same request is sent multiple times.

extStockId.description: The external ID of the stock. In most case, this can be used instead of the Front Systems internal stockId and storeId

SettlementId.description: A unique identifier for the settlement transaction.

BatchNumber.description: The batch number associated with this settlement, grouping multiple transactions.

StoreId.description: Identifier for the store where the transaction took place.

StoreName.description: The name of the store where the transaction took place.

PosId.description: Point of Sale identifier, specifying the terminal where the transaction was made.

PosName.description: The name of the Point of Sale terminal.

PersonName.description: The name of the person who conducted the transaction or settlement.

SettlementTimeStamp.description: The timestamp marking when the settlement was processed. Expected format is ISO 8601.

DateTimeFrom.description: Start time of the reporting period for this settlement. Expected format is ISO 8601.

DateTimeTo.description: End time of the reporting period for this settlement. Expected format is ISO 8601.

Currency.description: The currency code for all monetary values in this transaction, following the ISO 4217 standard.

GrossTotal.description: The total gross amount of the transaction before any deductions.

GrossDiscount.description: The total amount of discount applied to the transaction.

GrandTotal.description: The final amount after all discounts have been applied.

NetTotal.description: The net total of the transaction after VAT has been accounted for.

VATTotal.description: The total amount of VAT charged in this transaction.

VATArea1.description: VAT amount for the first VAT area or category.

VATArea2.description: VAT amount for the second VAT area or category.

VATArea3.description: VAT amount for the third VAT area or category.

OpeningCashBalance.description: The cash balance at the beginning of the settlement period.

ClosingCashBalance.description: The cash balance at the end of the settlement period.

Discrepancy.description: Any discrepancy in the cash balance from what was expected.

Comment.description: Any additional comments related to the settlement.

Cost.description: The cost associated with the transaction or settlement.

reportType.description: The type of report, indicated by a numerical code.

companyIdent.description: Identifier for the company, such as a tax or registration number.

companyName.description: The legal name of the company.

reportDate.description: The date when the report was generated. Expected format is ISO 8601.

reportTime.description: The time when the report was generated. Expected format is ISO 8601.

registerID.description: The identifier of the register from which the report is generated.

totalCashSaleAmnt.description: The total amount of cash sales.

artGroupID.description: The identifier for the article group.

artGroupNum.description: The numeric identifier for the article group.

artGroupAmnt.description: The amount associated with this article group.

reportArtGroups.description: A collection of article groups reported in the Z report.

paymentType.description: The type of payment, identified by a code or name.

paymentNum.description: A unique identifier for the payment transaction.

reportPayments.description: Details of payments made within the report.

paymentType.description: The type of payment made by the employee.

paymentNum.description: A unique number identifying the payment transaction by the employee.

paymentAmnt.description: The amount of the payment made by the employee.

vatCode.description: The VAT code used for the transaction.

cashSaleAmnt.description: The amount of the cash sale before VAT.

vatAmnt.description: The amount of VAT charged on the cash sale.

vatAmntTp.description: Type of VAT amount, possibly indicating a special category or treatment.

vatAmntTpSpecified.description: Boolean indicating if the VAT amount type is specified and relevant.

reportCashSalesVat.description: Details of VAT on cash sales.

reportOpeningChangeFloat.description: The amount of change available at the start of the reporting period.

reportReceiptNum.description: The number of receipts issued during the reporting period.

reportReceiptCopyNum.description: The number of receipt copies issued during the reporting period.

reportReceiptCopyAmnt.description: The total amount for all receipt copies issued.

reportReceiptProformaAmnt.description: The total amount for all proforma receipts issued during the reporting period.

reportReturnNum.description: The number of returns processed during the reporting period.

reportReturnAmnt.description: The total amount refunded for returns during the reporting period.

reportDiscountNum.description: The number of discounts applied during the reporting period.

reportDiscountAmnt.description: The total amount of discounts given during the reporting period.

reportVoidTransNum.description: The number of transactions voided during the reporting period.

reportVoidTransAmnt.description: The total amount of voided transactions during the reporting period.

priceInquiryGroup.description: The group or category for the price inquiry.

priceInquiryNum.description: The number of inquiries made within a particular group.

priceInquiryAmnt.description: The total amount inquired for within the group.

reportPriceInquiries.description: A record of price inquiries made during the reporting period.

reportReceiptDeliveryAmnt.description: The total amount for receipted deliveries during the reporting period.

reportTrainingAmnt.description: The total amount processed in training mode during the reporting period.

reportGrandTotalSales.description: The grand total of sales during the reporting period.

reportGrandTotalReturn.description: The grand total of returns during the reporting period.

reportGrandTotalSalesNet.description: The net grand total of sales after returns are accounted for.

SAFT_ZReport.description: Detailed Z report following the SAFT (Standard Audit File for Tax) format.

Withdrawals.description: List of cash withdrawals made during the reporting period.

Deposits.description: List of cash deposits made during the reporting period.

PType.description: The type of entry in the Z report.

Value.description: The value associated with the entry type.

ZReport.description: A detailed breakdown of Z report entries.

example.comment: This is a sale, a return would have negative qty and payment.

example.name: Acme Summer Sale
example.description: Get 20% off on all Acme products during the summer sale.

example.name: ACME Running Shoes
example.internalDescription: Trail running shoes with breathable mesh.

isNew.description: Indicates whether the product is new or existing.

errors.description: Key-value pairs of error messages related to the product.

errors.SizeSystem: There is not a sizesystem suitable for this product.
errors.GTIN 7123395309756: The size label FailingSize in GTIN 7123395309756 does not exists in sizesystem id 21454

promotionsOffers.name: Acme Summer Sale
promotionsOffers.description: Get 20% off on all Acme products during the summer sale.

pageSize.description: Paging. Number of variants* to include in the resultset before filtration and subsequent response (*not number of styles)

upt.description: Include products updated after this time

isWebAvailable.description: Only return products marked as available online

excludeDeleted.description: Used to actively choose to exclude deleted products from result. Included by default

includeAlternativeIdentifiers.description: IncludeAlternativeIdentifiers needs to be included in the filter to list all registered GTINS for products. Identifiers will only be included if pagesize is less than 2000 for perfomance reasons.

stockID.description: Specify which stockid the product is moved from

vat.description: in percentage. F.ex 0.25 or 25 will be interpreted as 25%
